title: heracleum brunonis
intro: Heracleum brunonis, also known as the Welsh angelica or Welsh cow parsley, is a biennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. Heracleum brunonis can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, although its usage and preparation should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
